words.definitionEn: to lead or be in charge of a meeting, ceremony, etc.
words.exampleEn: the presiding judge

words.definitionEn: the job of being president of a country or an organization; the period of time somebody holds this job
words.exampleEn: the current holder of the EU presidency

words.definitionEn: connected with the position or activities of a president
words.exampleEn: a presidential campaign/candidate/election

words.definitionEn: respected and admired as very important or of very high quality
words.exampleEn: a prestigious award

words.definitionEn: used to say that you think that something is probably true
words.exampleEn: Presumably this is where the accident happened.

words.definitionEn: to suppose that something is true, although you do not have actual proof
words.exampleEn: They are very expensive, I presume?

words.definitionEn: to exist or be very common at a particular time or in a particular place
words.exampleEn: prevail in something, We were horrified at the conditions prevailing in local prisons.

words.definitionEn: the fact of existing or being very common at a particular time or in a particular place
words.exampleEn: an increase in the prevalence of smoking among young people

words.definitionEn: the act of stopping something bad from happening
words.exampleEn: accident/crime prevention

words.definitionEn: an animal, a bird, etc. that is hunted, killed and eaten by another
words.exampleEn: The lion will often stalk its prey for hours.

words.definitionEn: the person who is in charge of a college or (in Scotland and Canada) a university
words.exampleEn: Peter Brown, principal of St John’s College

words.definitionEn: to wait for somebody/something
words.exampleEn: He is in custody awaiting trial.

words.definitionEn: the act of selling a business or an industry so that it is no longer owned by the government
words.exampleEn: There were fears that privatization would lead to job losses.

words.definitionEn: a special right or advantage that a particular person or group of people has
words.exampleEn: Education should be a universal right and not a privilege.

words.definitionEn: a complete and careful investigation of something
words.exampleEn: a police probe into the financial affairs of the company

words.definitionEn: to ask questions in order to find out secret or hidden information about somebody/something
words.exampleEn: probe (into something), He didn't like the media probing into his past.

words.definitionEn: difficult to deal with or to understand; full of problems; not certain to be successful
words.exampleEn: The situation is more problematic than we first thought.

words.definitionEn: the process of using a court to settle an argument or to deal with a complaint
words.exampleEn: court/legal/judicial proceedings

words.definitionEn: the money that you receive when you sell something or organize a performance, etc.; profits
words.exampleEn: She sold her car and bought a piano with the proceeds.

words.definitionEn: the treatment of raw material, food, etc. in order to change it, preserve it, etc.
words.exampleEn: the food processing industry

words.definitionEn: a machine, person or company that processes things
words.exampleEn: The company is Ireland's largest dairy processor.

words.definitionEn: to publicly and officially tell people about something important
words.exampleEn: proclaim something, The president proclaimed a state of emergency.

words.definitionEn: making goods or growing crops, especially in large quantities
words.exampleEn: highly productive farming land

words.definitionEn: the rate at which a worker, a company or a country produces goods, and the amount produced, compared with how much time, work and money is needed to produce them
words.exampleEn: high/improved/increased productivity

words.definitionEn: that makes or is likely to make money
words.exampleEn: a highly profitable business

words.definitionEn: very great; felt or experienced very strongly
words.exampleEn: profound changes in the earth’s climate

words.definitionEn: in a way that is appropriate to what has been done or said in a particular situation
words.exampleEn: We have to discover his plans and act accordingly.

words.definitionEn: an estimate or a statement of what figures, amounts, or events will be in the future, or what they were in the past, based on what is happening now
words.exampleEn: to make forward/backward projections of population figures

words.definitionEn: important or well known
words.exampleEn: a prominent politician

words.definitionEn: very obvious, easy to notice or strongly expressed
words.exampleEn: He walked with a pronounced limp.

words.definitionEn: ideas or statements that may be false or present only one side of an argument that are used in order to gain support for a political leader, party, etc.
words.exampleEn: enemy propaganda

words.definitionEn: an idea or a plan of action that is suggested, especially in business
words.exampleEn: I'd like to put a business proposition to you.

words.definitionEn: to officially charge somebody with a crime in court
words.exampleEn: The police decided not to prosecute.

words.definitionEn: the process of trying to prove in court that somebody is guilty of a crime (= of prosecuting them); the process of being officially charged with a crime in court
words.exampleEn: Prosecution for a first minor offence rarely leads to imprisonment.

words.definitionEn: a public official who charges somebody officially with a crime and prosecutes them in court
words.exampleEn: the public/state prosecutor

words.definitionEn: expected to do something or to become something
words.exampleEn: a prospective buyer

words.definitionEn: the state of being successful, especially in making money
words.exampleEn: Our future prosperity depends on economic growth.

words.definitionEn: providing or intended to provide protection
words.exampleEn: Workers should wear full protective clothing.

words.definitionEn: a system of fixed rules and formal behaviour used at official meetings, usually between governments
words.exampleEn: a breach of protocol

words.definitionEn: one of the areas that some countries are divided into with its own local government
words.exampleEn: the provinces of Canada

words.definitionEn: connected with one of the large areas that some countries are divided into, with its own local government
words.exampleEn: provincial assemblies/elections

words.definitionEn: the act of supplying somebody with something that they need or want; something that is supplied
words.exampleEn: housing provision

words.definitionEn: to cause a particular reaction or have a particular effect
words.exampleEn: The announcement provoked a storm of protest.

words.definitionEn: relating to psychiatry or to mental illnesses
words.exampleEn: psychiatric disorders

words.definitionEn: the regular beat of the heart as it sends blood around the body, that can be felt in different places, especially on the inside part of the wrist; the number of times the heart beats in a minute
words.exampleEn: a strong/weak pulse

words.definitionEn: a machine that is used to force liquid, gas or air into or out of something
words.exampleEn: (British English), a petrol pump

words.definitionEn: to make water, air, gas, etc. flow in a particular direction by using a pump or something that works like a pump
words.exampleEn: pump something (+ adv./prep.), The engine is used for pumping water out of the mine.

words.definitionEn: a hard hit made with the fist (= closed hand)
words.exampleEn: a punch in the face

words.definitionEn: to hit somebody/something hard with your fist (= closed hand)
words.exampleEn: punch somebody/something, He was kicked and punched as he lay on the ground.

words.definitionEn: everything that can be seen around an event or scene
words.exampleEn: The mountains provided a dramatic backdrop for our picnic.

words.definitionEn: a question, especially one asking for information or expressing a doubt about something
words.exampleEn: Our assistants will be happy to answer your queries.

words.definitionEn: a long search for something, especially for some quality such as happiness
words.exampleEn: quest for something, the quest for happiness/knowledge/truth

words.definitionEn: a limited number or amount of people or things that is officially allowed
words.exampleEn: to introduce a strict import quota on grain

words.definitionEn: help and support from somebody to do something; financial support for something
words.exampleEn: The police gave the proposals their full backing.

words.definitionEn: a system that uses radio waves to find the position and movement of objects, for example planes and ships, when they cannot be seen
words.exampleEn: They located the ship by radar.

words.definitionEn: relating to the most basic and important parts of something; complete and detailed
words.exampleEn: the need for radical changes in education

words.definitionEn: extra help or support that you can get if necessary
words.exampleEn: The police had backup from the army.

words.definitionEn: a feeling of violent anger that is difficult to control
words.exampleEn: His face was dark with rage.

words.definitionEn: a short surprise attack on an enemy by soldiers, ships or aircraft
words.exampleEn: to conduct/launch a raid

words.definitionEn: to visit a person or place without warning to look for criminals, illegal goods, drugs, etc.
words.exampleEn: The house was raided in the early hours.

words.definitionEn: a large public meeting, especially one held to support a particular idea or political party
words.exampleEn: to attend/hold a rally

words.definitionEn: to come together or bring people together in order to help or support somebody/something
words.exampleEn: rally around/behind somebody/something, The cabinet rallied behind the Prime Minister.

words.definitionEn: the position of somebody/something on a scale that shows how good or important they are in relation to other similar people or things, especially in sport
words.exampleEn: He has improved his ranking this season from 67th to 30th.

words.definitionEn: the crime of forcing somebody to have sex when they do not want it or are not able to agree to it
words.exampleEn: He was charged with rape.

words.definitionEn: to force somebody to have sex when they do not want it or are not able to agree to it
words.exampleEn: She was attacked and raped.

words.definitionEn: the relationship between two groups of people or things that is represented by two numbers showing how much larger one group is than the other
words.exampleEn: The school has a very high teacher-student ratio.

words.definitionEn: based on reason rather than emotions
words.exampleEn: a rational argument/choice/decision

words.definitionEn: a narrow line of light, heat or other energy
words.exampleEn: The last of the sun's rays shone on the grass.

words.definitionEn: quickly and without difficulty
words.exampleEn: All ingredients are readily available from your local store.

words.definitionEn: the process of becoming aware of something
words.exampleEn: As realization dawned, he went pale.

words.definitionEn: an area of activity, interest or knowledge
words.exampleEn: in the realm of something, in the realm of literature

words.definitionEn: at or near the back of something
words.exampleEn: front and rear windows

words.definitionEn: the back part of something
words.exampleEn: A trailer was attached to the rear of the truck.

words.definitionEn: the process of thinking about things in a logical way; opinions and ideas that are based on logical thinking
words.exampleEn: What is the reasoning behind this decision?

words.definitionEn: to say or do something that makes somebody less frightened or worried
words.exampleEn: reassure somebody (about something), They tried to reassure her, but she still felt anxious.

words.definitionEn: a person who fights against the government of their country
words.exampleEn: rebel forces

words.definitionEn: an attempt by some of the people in a country to change their government, using violence
words.exampleEn: in rebellion (against somebody/something), The north of the country rose in rebellion against the government.

words.definitionEn: a person who receives something
words.exampleEn: recipients of awards

words.definitionEn: money that somebody agrees to pay if a person accused of a crime does not appear at their trial. When bail has been arranged, the accused person is allowed to go free until the trial.
words.exampleEn: Can anyone put up bail for you?

words.definitionEn: the process of changing or improving the condition of something or the way it works; the process of putting something back into the state it was in before
words.exampleEn: the reconstruction of the educational system

words.definitionEn: sound or pictures that have been recorded on computer files or on CD, DVD, video, etc.
words.exampleEn: an audio recording

words.definitionEn: an occasion when all the people of a country can vote on an important issue
words.exampleEn: referendum on something, Switzerland decided to hold a referendum on joining the EU.

words.definitionEn: an image in a mirror, on a shiny surface, on water, etc.
words.exampleEn: He admired his reflection in the mirror.

words.definitionEn: change that is made to a social system, an organization, etc. in order to improve or correct it
words.exampleEn: a government committed to reform

words.definitionEn: to improve a system, an organization, a law, etc. by making changes to it
words.exampleEn: proposals to reform the social security system

words.definitionEn: shelter or protection from danger, trouble, etc.
words.exampleEn: A further 300 people have taken refuge in the US embassy.

words.definitionEn: an act of saying or showing that you will not do, give or accept something
words.exampleEn: the refusal of a request/an invitation/an offer

words.definitionEn: to get back something you no longer have, especially an ability or a quality
words.exampleEn: I struggled to regain some dignity.

words.definitionEn: paying no attention, even if the situation is bad or there are difficulties
words.exampleEn: The weather was terrible but we carried on regardless.

words.definitionEn: a method or system of government, especially one that has not been elected in a fair way
words.exampleEn: a fascist/totalitarian/military, etc. regime

words.definitionEn: a person or an organization that officially controls an area of business or industry and makes sure that it is operating fairly
words.exampleEn: Ofgas, the gas industry regulator

words.definitionEn: having the power to control an area of business or industry and make sure that it is operating fairly
words.exampleEn: regulatory bodies/authorities/agencies

words.definitionEn: the process of helping somebody to return to a normal, healthy life after they have been in prison or very ill
words.exampleEn: a drug rehabilitation centre

words.definitionEn: the period during which a king, queen, emperor, etc. rules
words.exampleEn: By the end of his reign, the vast empire was in decline.

words.definitionEn: to rule as king, queen, emperor, etc.
words.exampleEn: It was the first visit by a British reigning monarch to Russia.

words.definitionEn: the act of refusing to accept or consider something
words.exampleEn: Her proposal met with unanimous rejection.

words.definitionEn: a close connection with the subject you are discussing or the situation you are in
words.exampleEn: I don't see the relevance of your question.

words.definitionEn: the quality of being able to be trusted to do what somebody wants or needs
words.exampleEn: The incident cast doubt on her motives and reliability.

words.definitionEn: hesitating before doing something because you do not want to do it or because you are not sure that it is the right thing to do
words.exampleEn: He finally gave a reluctant smile.

words.definitionEn: the people, things or time that remain
words.exampleEn: I kept some of his books and gave away the remainder.

words.definitionEn: the parts of something that are left after the other parts have been used, eaten, removed, etc.
words.exampleEn: She fed the remains of her lunch to the dog.

words.definitionEn: the system of voting in writing and usually in secret; an occasion on which a vote is held
words.exampleEn: The chairperson is chosen by secret ballot.

words.definitionEn: a way of dealing with or improving an unpleasant or difficult situation
words.exampleEn: When the reservoir becomes blocked, the only remedy lies in cleaning the entire system.

words.definitionEn: something that makes you think about or remember somebody/something, that you have forgotten or would like to forget
words.exampleEn: reminder of something, The sheer size of the cathedral is a constant reminder of the power of religion.

words.definitionEn: the act of taking somebody/something away from a particular place
words.exampleEn: Clearance of the site required the removal of a number of trees.

words.definitionEn: to cause somebody/something to be in a particular state or condition
words.exampleEn: to render something harmless/useless/ineffective

words.definitionEn: to begin something again after it stopped or was interrupted
words.exampleEn: The army renewed its assault on the capital.

words.definitionEn: famous and respected
words.exampleEn: a renowned author

words.definitionEn: the amount of money that you pay to use something for a particular period of time
words.exampleEn: Telephone charges include line rental.

words.definitionEn: the act of replacing one thing with another, especially something that is newer or better
words.exampleEn: the replacement of worn car parts

words.definitionEn: according to what some people say
words.exampleEn: The band have reportedly decided to split up.

words.definitionEn: the fact of being responsible for your decisions or actions and expected to explain them when you are asked
words.exampleEn: proposals for greater police accountability

words.definitionEn: the act of presenting somebody/something in a particular way; something that shows or describes something
words.exampleEn: the negative representation of single mothers in the media

words.definitionEn: to make a copy of a picture, piece of text, etc.
words.exampleEn: It is illegal to reproduce these worksheets without permission from the publisher.

words.definitionEn: the act or process of producing babies, young animals or plants
words.exampleEn: sexual reproduction

words.definitionEn: a country that is governed by a president and politicians elected by the people and where there is no king or queen
words.exampleEn: newly independent republics

words.definitionEn: to look like or be similar to another person or thing
words.exampleEn: She closely resembles her sister.

words.definitionEn: to live in a particular place
words.exampleEn: He returned to Britain in 1939, having resided abroad for many years.

words.definitionEn: a house, especially a large or impressive one
words.exampleEn: a desirable family residence for sale (= for example, in an advertisement)

words.definitionEn: suitable for living in; consisting of houses rather than factories or offices
words.exampleEn: a quiet residential area

words.definitionEn: a small amount of something that remains at the end of a process
words.exampleEn: pesticide residues in fruit and vegetables

words.definitionEn: the act of giving up your job or position; the occasion when you do this
words.exampleEn: a letter of resignation

words.definitionEn: a long piece of cloth with a message on it that is carried between two poles or hung in a public place to show support for something
words.exampleEn: A huge banner over the street said ‘Welcome home’.

words.definitionEn: dislike of or opposition to a plan, an idea, etc.; the act of refusing to obey
words.exampleEn: As with all new ideas it met with resistance.

words.definitionEn: belonging or relating separately to each of the people or things already mentioned
words.exampleEn: They are each recognized specialists in their respective fields.

words.definitionEn: in the same order as the people or things already mentioned
words.exampleEn: Julie and Mark, aged 17 and 19 respectively

words.definitionEn: the work of repairing and cleaning an old building, a painting, etc. so that its condition is as good as it originally was
words.exampleEn: restoration work

words.definitionEn: a rule, a fact, an idea, etc. that limits or controls what people can do
words.exampleEn: The government has imposed export restraints on some products.

words.definitionEn: if you resume an activity, or if it resumes, it begins again or continues after being interrupted
words.exampleEn: resume (something), to resume talks/negotiations

words.definitionEn: a movement away from a place or an enemy because of danger or defeat
words.exampleEn: Napoleon’s retreat from Moscow

words.definitionEn: not covered by any clothes
words.exampleEn: She likes to walk around in bare feet.

words.definitionEn: to move away from a place or an enemy because you are in danger or because you have been defeated
words.exampleEn: The army was forced to retreat after suffering heavy losses.

words.definitionEn: to bring or get something back, especially from a place where it should not be
words.exampleEn: retrieve something from somebody/something, She bent to retrieve her comb from the floor.

words.definitionEn: a fact that people are made aware of, especially one that has been secret and is surprising
words.exampleEn: revelation about/concerning something, startling/sensational revelations about her private life

words.definitionEn: something that you do in order to make somebody suffer because they have made you suffer
words.exampleEn: revenge for something, She is seeking revenge for the murder of her husband.
